req.title: Hr-Admin deletes timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a happy-path delete by an authenticated hr-admin (EP – valid partition), (2) an unauthenticated attempt that must yield 401 (NEG), (3) a forbidden role attempt yielding 403 (DT – role/auth decision table), (4) a boundary case where the timesheet ID is the minimum valid value (BVA), and (5) a state-transition case verifying the timesheet moves from EXISTING → DELETED and an audit record is written (STC). Together these cover the positive, negative, and boundary requirements and all stated error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_valid_timesheet_success
preconditions: A timesheet record exists with a known ID
actions: Click the Delete button and confirm the dialog
Capture the response status and returned entity id
expected: Response body contains the deleted timesheet entity id
An audit record is written for the deletion
trace: R-WEB-019 – positive outcome: timesheet deleted, audit written, 200/201 returned
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_deletes_valid_timesheet_success(hr_admin_page, existing_timesheet, api_client):
    page = hr_admin_page
    ts_id = existing_timesheet["id"]

    page.goto("/timesheets")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='timesheet-row-{ts_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    response = <capture_last_api_response>(page, "/timesheets")
    assert response["status"] in (200, 201)
    assert response["body"]["id"] == ts_id

    page.reload()
    assert page.locator(f"[data-testid='timesheet-row-{ts_id}']").count() == 0

    audit = api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=DELETE")
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit.json()["records"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_timesheet
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A timesheet record exists with a known ID
actions: Ensure no authentication cookie or token is present
Send DELETE request directly to /timesheets/<id> without credentials
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_timesheet(api_client, existing_timesheet):
    ts_id = existing_timesheet["id"]

    response = api_client.delete(
        f"/timesheets/{ts_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": ""}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 401

    still_exists = api_client.get(
        f"/timesheets/{ts_id}",
        headers=<hr_admin_auth_header>
    )
    assert still_exists.status_code == 200

    audit = api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=DELETE",
        headers=<hr_admin_auth_header>
    )
    assert len(audit.json()["records"]) == 0

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_timesheet
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A timesheet record exists with a known ID
actions: Log in as a user with role 'employee' via <employee_login_fixture>
Attempt to delete the target timesheet via UI or API
trace: R-WEB-019 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_timesheet(employee_api_client, existing_timesheet, hr_admin_api_client):
    ts_id = existing_timesheet["id"]

    response = employee_api_client.delete(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 403

    still_exists = hr_admin_api_client.get(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert still_exists.status_code == 200

    audit = hr_admin_api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=DELETE"
    )
    assert len(audit.json()["records"]) == 0

tests.name: test_delete_timesheet_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: A timesheet record exists whose ID is the minimum allowable positive integer (1)
actions: Inspect the response status and body
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
Timesheet with id=1 is removed from the system
An audit record for entity_id=1 action=DELETE is written
trace: R-WEB-019 – BVA on timesheet ID: lower boundary of valid ID range
tests.executable:
def test_delete_timesheet_with_minimum_valid_id(hr_admin_api_client, ensure_timesheet_id_1_exists):
    ts_id = 1

    response = hr_admin_api_client.delete(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert response.json()["id"] == ts_id

    gone = hr_admin_api_client.get(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert gone.status_code == 404

    audit = hr_admin_api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=DELETE"
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit.json()["records"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_timesheet_transitions_to_deleted_state_and_audit_written
preconditions: A timesheet exists in SUBMITTED state
actions: Verify the timesheet is in SUBMITTED state via GET /timesheets/<id>
Navigate to /timesheets and delete the SUBMITTED timesheet
Query the audit log for the DELETE action
expected: Before deletion: timesheet status is SUBMITTED
HTTP delete response status is 200 or 201
Audit log contains one DELETE record for the entity
trace: R-WEB-019 – STC: SUBMITTED → DELETED state transition; audit written on success
tests.executable:
def test_timesheet_transitions_to_deleted_state_and_audit_written(hr_admin_api_client, submitted_timesheet):
    ts_id = submitted_timesheet["id"]

    pre = hr_admin_api_client.get(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["status"] == "SUBMITTED"

    delete_resp = hr_admin_api_client.delete(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert delete_resp.json()["id"] == ts_id

    post = hr_admin_api_client.get(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert post.status_code == 404

    audit = hr_admin_api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=DELETE"
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    records = audit.json()["records"]
    assert len(records) == 1
    assert records[0]["action"] == "DELETE"
